name: Jalbert
bio:
Physical Appearance: Redguard male. Wears standard necromancer's robes along with some relatively rare apparel: a dark shirt, black wide pants, leather bracers, cowhide shoes, and a copper ring - a mixture of practical necromancer garb and unusual accessories.
Race: Redguard
Personality Keywords: exiled, grateful, hostile, dedicated, sleepless
Role: Jalbert is a necromancer who was exiled from his native Daggerfall for practicing necromancy. While grave robbing, he was discovered by a friend in the Guard, Captain Aluc Cardius, forcing him to flee. He now hides in the Ayleid ruin of Vilverin, dedicating himself entirely to his dark practices.
Location: Vilverin Sel Sancremathi, a chamber within the Ayleid ruin of Vilverin. Jalbert has made this ancient ruin his new home and workspace.
Daily Routine: Jalbert spends all his time in Vilverin Sel Sancremathi, never leaving and never sleeping despite having a bedroll available. His dedication to his necromantic work appears absolute.
Equipment: Jalbert carries a dagger (always of the best possible quality for his level) and frequently carries a staff as well. He has a good chance of carrying leveled poison. He relies heavily on necromancer spells, using the strongest possible spells for his skill level. He carries the Vilverin Sepulcher key.
His Chamber: In his new home, Jalbert keeps various items: a sacred lotus flower, sacred lotus seeds, a mortar and pestle for grinding ingredients, two lockpicks, nine gold coins, an Ayleid reliquary, a sack, and a bedroll. A fine iron longsword and shield lay next to a skeleton on one of the slabs.
The Undelivered Letter: Jalbert has written a letter to Captain Aluc Cardius, the guard who discovered him grave robbing. The letter apologizes to Aluc and thanks him, though it remains undelivered. This suggests complicated feelings about the friend whose discovery forced his exile.
Exile from Daggerfall: Jalbert was forced to flee his homeland after being caught practicing necromancy. The fact that it was a friend in the Guard who discovered him adds a personal dimension to his exile.
Personality - The Grateful Exile: Despite being forced into exile and hiding in ancient ruins, Jalbert has written an apologetic and grateful letter to Captain Aluc Cardius, the friend whose discovery led to his flight from Daggerfall. This reveals someone who maintains appreciation even toward the person responsible for his exile, suggesting either genuine friendship or understanding that Aluc had no choice but to report him. He's dedicated himself completely to his necromantic work, never sleeping and spending all his time in the chamber. However, his high aggression means he attacks visitors on sight, showing he's protective of his new sanctuary and unwilling to risk discovery again. The presence of the undelivered letter suggests he may wrestle with his circumstances - grateful to have escaped but perhaps isolated and unable to maintain connections from his past life.
author: Bethesda
